answer: The script became progressively simpler and more angular over time. From Sumerian to Early Babylonian, pictographic elements were reduced and stylized. From Early Babylonian to Late Babylonian, glyphs became more compact and abstract. From Late Babylonian to Assyrian, forms became even more simplified with fewer wedge strokes, making writing faster and more efficient.
reasoning: Observing the evolution pattern across all rows shows consistent simplification: complex pictographic forms → more abstract wedge combinations → simplified angular forms with fewer strokes
